title: haloragis micrantha
intro: Haloragis micrantha, also known as small water milfoil, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its small white or pinkish flowers and distinctive leaf shape. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Haloragis micrantha include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, relieve congestion, and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Haloragis micrantha can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
